subject: Add your name to call for immediate action on gun violence prevention

body:
We need to stand with Senate Democrats | Friend --

This week, Republicans in Congress asked for a moment of silence for the victims of the shooting in Orlando. Democrats in the House of Representatives refused. They would not stand silent, they said, about the work it's going to take to end the nation's gun violence epidemic.

Then, Senator Chris Murphy of Connecticut and other Senate Democrats launched a filibuster, saying they would not yield the floor until Republicans agreed to debate legislation that would keep guns out of the hands of suspected terrorists and require comprehensive background checks for gun purchases.

They're going to hold the floor of the Senate and keep talking until Republicans listen -- or until they can no longer stay on their feet.

**We need to stand with them -- will you add your name right now to stand with Hillary and Democrats in Congress to call for immediate action on gun violence prevention?**
[](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=11&ms=MjU3NTA4NzMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODIyNDA1NzY4S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thanks,

Christina

Christina Reynolds
Deputy Communications Director
Hillary for America

Peter,

We've made a lot of progress together in Ohio and we can't afford to go backwards.

**That is why I am making re-electing my friend Rob Portman to the United States Senate a top priority.** [**Please show your support right now with a quick online donation.**](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/35934869:sa0OIwYNW:m:1:1353064270:32329F4D83648A29343D23D7275F84C3:r)

Peter, Ohio desperately needs more leaders like Rob in the Senate. Rob and I have been friends for more than twenty years. We've worked together to combat Ohio's drug epidemic, restore fiscal responsibility and grow our economy.

Before I took office Rob's opponent was governor of Ohio. During his time in office Ohio lost more than 350,000 jobs, we ranked 48th in job creation, and our rainy day fund was drained to just 89 cents. Our country can't afford that kind of management.

However, Rob's opponents will stop at nothing to try to win in November. In fact, they've already reserved more than $20 million in misleading ads attacking Rob!

That is why I am asking for your help, Peter.

[**You are one of my strongest supporters and I know I can count on you. Our job isn't done yet, Peter. name: Justice for Governor
subject: Thoughts on the budget
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Justice for Governor
body:
**Justice: Special Session for Budget Needs Reform**
_Due to wasted time and taxpayer money during this special session on the budget, Justice calls for changes_
After the Republican-led legislature wasted $595,000 during the special session on the budget, Jim Justice called for reforms to avoid another expensive and drawn-out process in the future. This year the West Virginia legislature billed taxpayers $35,000 a day during the special session for the budget.
Jim wants to limit lawmaker pay to a total of five work-days for any special session dealing with the budget and ban all fundraising while the legislature is in session. If it takes longer than five days to pass a budget during a special session, the lawmakers will not get paid for the extra time it takes.
"I am too impatient to watch the politicians bicker for months and months and see nothing get done," Jim said. "The budget mess in Charleston proves that we need to impose a tighter timeline on the legislature because they wasted too much money and created uncertainty for our families. On the farm, I've put in many 18-hour days, and I think lawmakers should do the same."
Justice has called for a series of [ethics reforms](https://act.myngp.com/el/460744402470832128/3186718449699522560), including banning fundraising while the legislature is in session so lobbyists and associations that represent various industries can't be extorted for campaign cash.
Justice added,"No politician should be fundraising instead of doing the job they were elected to do."
**Bill Cole and the Republican leadership wasted $595,000 during the recent special session on the budget. Here's what that means for West Virginia families:**
* 125 additional PROMISE scholarships that could have helped WV students (Register-Herald, _5.15.16_)
* 18,593 more potholes could have been fixed (WCHS, _5.23.16_)
* 18 new teachers could have been added (Preston County News & Journal, _10.9.15_)
* 14 new state troopers could have been hired (_West Virginia State Police_)
* 87 students could have had a full year of tuition covered at Marshall University (_Marshall.edu_)
* 19 students could have attended West Virginia University for four years(_WVU.edu_)
* 26 corrections officers could have been hired (Metro News _6.26.15_)
* 396 addicts could have been treated in a recovery program for one month (WCHS, _5.23.16_)
**_Lawmakers Are Getting Behind Jim 's Proposal To Reform Special Session on Budget_**
**Senate Minority Whip John Unger:**
_" Jim Justice is right; there is no reason we can't cap special session pay to five days when we are working on the budget. This year the Republican leadership dragged their feet, and taxpayer money was wasted. Reforming special sessions related to the budget will speed up the process. _
_West Virginians need to know that the legislature is 100% focused on doing its job, and not out fundraising and pandering to special interests. "_
**House Minority Leader Tim Miley:**
_" After the $595,000 in taxpayer money that was wasted this year by the Republican-led legislature during the budget impasse it's clear we need reform. West Virginians expect us to work hard around the clock to get the job done, and capping pay to 5 days will help us finish the budget faster. If no work is being done, no legislator should get paid. If working people don't get paid if they don't perform their jobs; why should we as legislators?"_
**Senator Doug Facemire:**
_" I support Jim's idea of only paying legislators for the first five days of a special session on the budget. If we had that in place this year, the budget would have been done a long time ago and saved the taxpayers some money. It will also cut down on the political games."_
**Delegate Mike Caputo:**
_" While working families are struggling it's irresponsible to burn through money. It's a shame that Bill Cole's failed leadership cost the taxpayers so much money during the special session. We need a stricter deadline and to act with more urgency when dealing with the budget. Cole's focus on fundraising during the session really slowed things down."_
Help spread the word about Jim and his campaign. Follow us on [Facebook](https://act.myngp.com/el/460744402470832128/3258776043737450496) and [Twitter](https://act.myngp.com/el/460744402470832128/3330833637775378432)

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/460744402470832128/3402891231813306368?refcode=home)

Paid for by Justice for Governor. Public employees please disregard.

Donald Trump is unfit to be president | Friend --

In the wake of the deadliest mass shooting in United States history, the GOP nominee for president didn't pledge to stand with the LGBT community, didn't make a distinction between Muslims and jihadists, didn't lay out a measured and thoughtful plan to prevent tragedies like this from happening again.

Instead, he said I told you so. He took to the internet and fired off a tweet: “Appreciate the congrats for being right on radical Islamic terrorism, I don't want congrats, I want toughness & vigilance. We must be smart!”

We don’t need your self-congratulatory victory lap at a time of mourning, Donald. Our nation needs time to heal and reflect. We need a leader who can calmly and rationally lead us out of the darkness we find ourselves in -- not one who creates light with kerosene and a match.

name: TEAM COLE
subject: COLE TO JUSTICE: Stop Games, Offer Fiscal Plan
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF COLE
body:
| |
---
| Friend --
Republican Gubernatorial nominee Bill Cole called on Jim Justice to stop throwing political stones, and instead offer voters his plan to move West Virginia forward and solve our state’s fiscal crisis without shutting down state government. Jim Justice offered no plan close the $271 million budget deficit in the budget lawmakers just passed. He has only made empty promises that he wouldn’t cut spending or increase revenue. He [offered](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fvid.me%2F1Jv4&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fiscal_plan&n=1) a silly stop-gap solution to audition for a spot on the reality TV show _Shark Tank_. “Jim Justice can’t have it both ways,” said Cole. “I worked hard as Senate President to begin to control the growth of state government, which has increased more than 56 percent during the last 16 years. Jim Justice offered no plan, which would have resulted in a government shutdown that was unacceptable to the citizens of West Virginia.” **The Cole Led Budget:**
* Began to rein in the growth of state government with $122 million in cuts while shoring up PEIA, continuing PROMISE Scholarships, guaranteeing Medicaid payments to providers, and preserving many key economic development programs.

* Identified $40 million in appropriated but unspent money, and prevented bureaucrats from wasting it by applying it to the Fiscal Year 2017 deficit.

* Rejected Governor Earl Ray Tomblin’s call for almost $300 million in tax hikes.

* Provided needed revenue in the form of a modest increase in the tax on tobacco, only after it became clear the Governor wouldn’t sign a budget without new revenue.

* Used a modest amount of the state’s Rainy Day Fund, which prevented a large reduction that would have jeopardized the state’s bond rating.

In a news release yesterday, Jim Justice [engaged](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fjusticeforwv.com%2Fjustice-special-session-for-budget-needs-reform%2F&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fiscal_plan&n=2) in more political gamesmanship and stone throwing by making false claims about Bill Cole’s dedication to solving the state’s fiscal crises. Jim Justice said he grew impatient about the legislative process, but at the same time, he has no problem being patient in [paying](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.wvgazettemail.com%2Farticle%2F20160414%2FGZ01%2F160419686&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fiscal_plan&n=3) his mounting tax debts that would go to fund schools and critical local government services. Jim Justice claims he is used to working 18-hour days, but he he[ failed](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.courier-journal.com%2Fstory%2Ftech%2Fscience%2Fenvironment%2F2016%2F06%2F06%2Fpike-slide-justice-mine-damages-homes%2F85502628%2F&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fiscal_plan&n=4) to step up and meet the obligations of an agreement to clean up one of his strip mine sites in Kentucky that recently resulted in a massive mudslide that destroyed the homes of innocent people. “While the state has a budget that offers fiscal certainty without a massive tax increase on hard working West Virginia families, the next Governor will walk into office in January 2017 [facing](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwvmetronews.com%2F2016%2F06%2F14%2Ftomblin-not-pointing-fingers-after-budget-passage%2F&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fiscal_plan&n=5) an at least $250 million budget deficit,” said Cole. “I am committed to continue to rein in the out-of-control growth of our state government. There are millions of dollars in cuts to the budget that only a Governor with experience and determination can make.” “Jim Justice needs to stop throwing political stones from The Greenbrier and come forward and offer voters his plan to solve our state’s fiscal crisis – specifically the $250 million deficit in next year’s budget,” challenged Cole. “If he remains silent, voters can only assume that he will continue to make empty promises and then force the largest tax increase in history that West Virginians cannot afford.” TEAM COLE

name: Amanda Curtis
subject: the un-Trump
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Steve Bullock - Democrat, P
body:
---
Friend,

Donald Trump is a reckless, mean-spirited, sexist and racist bully -- and he's an actual nominee for president of the United States. Greg Gianforte has lobbied in favor of discrimination, criticized seniors for relying on Social Security, filed a lawsuit against the state of Montana to block stream access, rolled out tax giveaways that would drain our budget and is backing Trump -- and he's the GOP nominee for governor of Montana.

**So amid the constant disheartening news this election cycle, Steve 's leadership stands out.** By bringing people together, he's expanded access to health care for tens of thousands of Montanans, protected public lands, cut taxes for small businesses, made record investments in public schools, stood up for women's rights to make our own healthcare decisions, maintained a tuition freeze at our colleges and universities...it's a long list. And because of Steve's level-headed, responsible approach, Montana is ranked as the most fiscally prudent state in the entire country.

Our (over-heated) political climate could use more leaders like Steve. That's why I'm going to be working as hard as possible to reelect him this year and I hope you'll join me. On **June 22nd Steve faces his first fundraising deadline of the general election. He 's $7,854 short. [Please chip in $15 to help him out.](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=Isjijg%2ByLI9F%2F6aVj7tTNBgzngjMKeQU)**

I'm sick of the ugly rhetoric playing out this election year. It's not how we do things here in Montana. I'm proud to think of Steve as the un-Trump -- in contrast to stoking hostility and discord, he's laser-focused on delivering for Montana families.
Please join me in standing up for civility and solutions. I think we could use a whole lot more of that right now. **[Make sure Steve's general election fundraising stays on track and donate $15 now.](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=1ve7rkfOn%2F8p5qWkes%2BQjxgzngjMKeQU)**

Thanks,
Amanda Curtis

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Something more profound
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
The soul of our state is at stake. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=OTA2ODg4MwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTQxNTg1MTM4S0&mt=1&rt=0)
| Friend, every election is a choice. A choice between candidates, parties, and ideas. I firmly believe that this election is also about something more profound: the very values that define our country, and our state. When Donald Trump preaches hate, we must be passionate advocates for compassion. When Donald Trump encourages violence, we must fight for inclusion and a welcoming spirit that lifts up our communities. And when Republicans across Washington state, including my opponent, refuse to disavow Donald Trump's vile campaign, we must build a grassroots army to fight back with a simple message: Enough is enough -- in Washington we stand on the side of hope. That's why I've set up a Stop Trump Challenge for our campaign, it's a bold goal: let's raise $25,000 before June 18th to send a forceful message that Trump-style politics are not welcome in Washington state. When our campaign is strong, Democrats in Washington are strong. We're better than hate. We're better than Trump. [**Will you chip in $5 to be part of our Stop Trump Challenge?**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=14&ms=OTA2ODg4MwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTQxNTg1MTM4S0&mt=2&rj=OTQxNTg1MTM4S0&rt=0) [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=OTA2ODg4MwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTQxNTg1MTM4S0&mt=1&rt=0) Donald Trump is not a typical Republican candidate, and this won't be a typical election. Fighting hate with hope will take a great effort, including a bold grassroots campaign, smart use of digital strategies, and a campaign that reaches throughout Washington with our message. That's why meeting our Stop Trump Challenge goal is so critical. At the heart of my work over the last three years is the belief that our state is better when we're united in a common progressive vision. I'm committed to continue fighting for a Washington that lives up to its progressive values. To do it, I need your help. [**We must win this November because the soul of our country and our state are at stake. That 's why I'm asking you to take part in the Stop Trump Challenge today.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=OTA2ODg4MwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTQxNTg1MTM4S0&mt=1&rt=0) Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [**DONATE »**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTA2ODg4MwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTQxNTg1MTM4S0&mt=1&rt=0)

name: Bob Fertik, Democrats.com
subject: Irrigating food with oil field waste?
body:
---

Dear Activist, California's Central Valley produces a great deal of the fruits and vegetables sold across the U.S. But did you know that oil field wastewater is being used to irrigate crops in two water districts in the region? Mandarin oranges, carrots and grapes are just some of the crops grown in areas that are selling farmers wastewater from oil drilling operations — and it's unclear to what extent this toxic water could be impacting the food we eat. **[Please join Food& Water Watch in urging Gov. Jerry Brown to stop oil wastewater irrigation on our food.](http://mail.democrats.com/CT00127201MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2016-06-16)** Thanks for all you do! Bob Fertik

|
---
| [ ](http://mail.democrats.com/CT00127202MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2016-06-16) |
| Fruits and Vegetables Grown With Oil Field Wastewater?
[Tell California's Governor Brown This Needs to Stop!](http://mail.democrats.com/CT00127203MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2016-06-16) |
---
Mmm, Methylene Chloride?
No Thanks! [ ](http://mail.democrats.com/CT00127204MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2016-06-16) [Say NO to Oil Field
Wastewater on Your Food!
](http://mail.democrats.com/CT00127205MTAwMzY3Mjc=.HTML?D=2016-06-16)
Dear Activist,

California's Central Valley produces fruits and vegetables for much of the United States, but _did you know they may be grown with wastewater from oil field operations_ — even some organic brands?

Last year, our friends at Water Defense revealed that the Cawelo Water District was **accepting oil wastewater and selling it to its agricultural water users**. But Cawelo wasn't alone.

**New Food & Water Watch research shows that this practice is more widespread than previously realized,** impacting the North Kern Water Storage District as well.

**Think using an oil field waste product to grow our food is horrifying? Tell Governor Brown to do something about it!**

Even after treatment, oil field wastewater can contain harmful, toxic chemicals, including acetone, methylene chloride and benzene, so the idea that it's being used to grow our food is ludicrous.

**Just how big a problem is this?** California grows**83% of fresh carrots** produced in the U.S. — and Kern County, CA, is a leading producer. **Eighty-eight percent of all grapes** produced in the U.S. are grown in California, with Kern County a leader in table grape production. And **mandarin oranges** , like Halos? Kern County's a top producer there too. Odds are, something you know and like at the store may have been irrigated with something nasty.

Brands who buy some of their product from farmers receiving water from the Cawelo or North Kern water districts include:

Sunview Raisins and Grapes
Trinchero Family Estates (makers of Sutter Home wines)
Halos mandarins (formerly known as Cuties)
The Wine Group (makers of Cupcake and Fish Eye wines)

**When you look at just how much of our food is potentially impacted by this practice, it quickly becomes clear that we can't shop our way around the problem.**

**We need to halt the practice of irrigating food crops with oil field wastewater — and California Governor Brown can do just that.**

It's not often that we ask you to sign a national petition directed at a state governor, but this issue isn't just a local concern. **What's being allowed by California regulators has big implications for food across the country. It's time for all of us, Californian or not, to demand that this practice is ended.**

Food production should not be the oil and gas industry's shortcut for getting rid of its industrial waste. **Governor Brown, protect our food and fix this oily mess.**

Thanks for taking action,

Amanda Byrnes
Online Organizer
Food & Water Watch
act(at)fwwatch(dot)org

| **_Washington Post:_ Senate Democrats end marathon filibuster, announce Republicans agree to gun control vote** Friend -- I've had enough. How much longer will Americans have to wait for Congress to act? How many more mass shootings must happen before Congress puts partisan politics aside and passes commonsense measures that the majority of Americans agree on to curb gun violence? Orlando, our state, and the LGBT community are still reeling from this weekend's senseless tragedy, the deadliest mass shooting in U.S. history. 49 innocent people are dead and 53 are wounded, many of them Hispanic and LGBT. Our thoughts and prayers are insufficient. Floridians -- and all Americans -- expect their elected leaders to work to end the epidemic of gun violence. To keep our country and all its citizens safe we need to:
* Close the terrorist gun loophole -- suspected terrorists shouldn't be able to legally purchase guns
* Mandate background checks for all gun purchases
* Ban high-capacity magazines and keep assault weapons out of the hands of dangerous people

It's the time for action, and if I were in the Senate, I would proudly stand with Senator Chris Murphy and Senate Democrats in support of their filibuster to force Mitch McConnell's hand on this critical issue.

Early this morning, after almost 15 hours, their courageous filibuster concluded with a promise from the GOP to hold a vote on background checks and the terrorist loophole. It's the very least they can do in the wake of Orlando.

[**Will you add your name to stand with me and Senate Democrats to end gun violence?**](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2c7e/738133e4/3683db40/7f829abd/2706756269/VEsE/ "https://www.murphyforflorida.com/landing/e160616/?subsource=ngpattre160616")

Sincerely,

Patrick |

subject: One Year Ago: The Start of a Movement
body:
Trump - Make America Great Again!
| |
---
| | [](https://donaldtrump.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.donaldjtrump.com%2F&e=8ff4c67fd343da79b63c0de3e94ed779&utm_source=donaldtrump&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=anniversary&n=1)

\- June 16th, 2016 -
# **One Year Ago: The Start Of a Movement**
| Dear Peter, A year ago today, many of you joined me at Trump Tower in calling for real change in Washington, D.C. I am proud to say that with your support we are well on our way to Making America Great Again! Since the moment I rode down the escalator on June 16th, 2015 and decided to run for president, my campaign has been filled with nothing but excitement, dedication, and passion to change our country for the better – and this is only the beginning of our movement! Yesterday in Atlanta, Georgia we had thousands of loving supporters. Earlier in the campaign we had a record-setting rally in Mobile, Alabama with over 30,000 attendees who joined our movement to Make America Great Again. I am honored and proud to lead our fight to put America first. This past year, I have traveled this great country and met many incredible Americans at our rallies. The Republican primary began with 17 experienced and talented candidates for president. Following the debate at the Ronald Reagan Library on September 16th, your overwhelming support showed that our campaign has the message that resonates with hard-working Americans and that the DC-centric way of thinking is no longer capable of leading our country. My campaign has been one of honesty and substance. We have created much-needed dialogue around the important issues facing our country. You’ve seen my leadership in setting the agenda this whole political season on both sides of the aisle. If I weren't running for president we wouldn’t be talking about illegal immigration. We wouldn’t be talking about the drugs pouring in and the bad trade deals costing American jobs. We wouldn’t have called the terrorist attack in Orlando for what it is - radical Islamic terrorism. The support of many wonderful Americans like you led to resounding victories in the Republican primary race, in which we beat the rigged political system and biased media with landslide victories. Our success began on February 9th with an emphatic win in New Hampshire and reached a high point with our victory in South Carolina on February 20th. We continued our success in Florida on March 15th followed by my home state of New York on April 19th – both huge turning points in our campaign. We had some tough losses along the way but with your support, we were able to bounce back and sweep the Connecticut, Delaware, Maryland, Pennsylvania and Rhode Island primaries on April 26th, along with other monumental wins throughout the primary season. The highlight was our win in Indiana on May 3rd - which made us the presumptive Republican nominee! The strong vote of confidence that the American voters have placed in our message of Making America Great Again has buoyed us to many victories and is truly heartwarming. While I am thankful for the phenomenal success we have enjoyed in the past year, it is just the beginning! I could not be more thrilled to be your presumptive Republican Presidential Nominee and to officially accept the GOP Nomination at the Convention in July. We must continue to strive towards beating Crooked Hillary in November and Making ALL of America Great Again! Thank you again for your support. Best Wishes,
**Donald J. Trump**

name: Senator Chris Murphy
subject: I'll never forget the scene from Newtown
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Why I filibustered for gun violence prevention | Friend --

I was the Congressman from Newtown, Connecticut when tragedy struck Sandy Hook Elementary School on December 14th, 2012. As long as I live, I’ll never forget the scene at the firehouse when twenty-six families were told they would not be reunited with their children and loved ones.

For years, Congress has failed those families and the victims of gun violence all across the country.

That is why yesterday, starting at 11:21 a.m., I decided to stand on the Senate floor and talk for as long as I could about the need to prevent gun violence. And with the help of more than 30 of my colleagues, we demanded immediate action to make our communities safer from gun violence:

I can tell you that as the hours progressed, my colleagues heard your calls for change grow louder and louder. And after almost fifteen hours of filibustering, I was proud to be able to announce that Republicans had agreed to our demand for a vote on legislation that would keep guns out of the hands of suspected terrorists and require comprehensive background checks for gun purchases.

That is just one step, and our fight is far from over. But there are millions of voices out there who believe like we do that now is the time for action. And if we keep making ourselves heard, we will save lives.

**Add your voice to Hillary Clinton’s and mine to demand Congress take immediate action to make our communities safer from gun violence:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=MjU3NjE5NzYS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODIyNTYzMDkwS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Whether it's shootings fueled by racism, rage against women, homophobia, extremist ideologies, or severe mental illness, I shudder to think what it says about us as a nation if we don't even attempt to make a good faith effort to end this carnage. We need to re-think whether or not that's something we are willing to allow. And like President Obama said on Sunday, doing nothing is a choice, too.

So I am going to stand with Hillary Clinton and continue this fight until Congress has done everything it can to make our communities safer from gun violence.

Thank you for adding your voice to ours.

Chris Murphy
U.S. Senator, Connecticut
